hero.headline: DIR-3 KYC Filing for Directors & DIN Holders
hero.subtext: Ensure your DIN remains active. MCA-compliant filing with Professionals Law – fast, accurate, and penalty-free.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: DIR-3 KYC is a mandatory compliance which has been introduced by Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) for all directors and designated partners who have been allotted Director Identification Number (DIN) pursuant to the second proviso to sub-section 1 of section 153 of the Companies Act, 2013 and Rule 12A of the Companies (Appointment and Qualification of Directors) Rules, 2014. Every Director who has been allotted DIN on or before the end of the financial year and whose DIN is in ‘Approved’ status, would be mandatorily required to file form DIR-3 KYC to update KYC details with the MCA. Professionals Law makes it easy for directors to meet their yearly compliance requirement by taking care of the entire DIR-3 KYC Filing and it is filed properly at the right time, eliminating the chances of penalties and DIN being deactivated.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: DIR-3 KYC is required for KYC of director with MCA and keeping the MCA records clean and updated with correct information. It also aims at preventing misuse of Director Identification Numbers (DIN) and promoting good corporate governance. By establishing the identity of persons who control companies and LLPs, this compliance process adds to the reality, responsibility and transparency of corporate control. Using Professionals Law ensures that directors can have peace of mind that their KYC obligations have been achieved without fuss.

body.title: Prevents Deactivation of DIN
body.description: Timely filing of DIR-3 KYC ensures your DIN remains active and valid, enabling you to serve on boards and file documents with the ROC without restrictions.

body.title: Avoids Penalties
body.description: Filing on time saves directors from heavy penalties of ₹5,000 and other late fees imposed by the MCA for non-compliance.

body.title: Strengthens Corporate Credibility
body.description: Directors who are compliant with statutory requirements enhance the credibility and legal standing of the company.

body.title: Protects Against Fraud
body.description: KYC verification prevents the misuse of director identities and strengthens security in the corporate ecosystem.

body.title: Mandatory for Future Appointments
body.description: A valid, active DIN is essential for appointments, resignations and any future filings involving the director.

categories.applicantType: The following individuals are required to file DIR-3 KYC:
criteria: DIN Holders (even if DIN is inactive) - Mandatory
Individuals allotted DIN before the financial year-end - Mandatory
Whether you are a first-time director or an experienced professional, Professionals Law ensures your KYC compliance is always up to date.

documents: Current Residential Address Proof (utility bill, bank statement, etc.)
Personal Mobile Number and Email ID (for OTP verification)
Digital Signature Certificate (DSC) of the director

body.step: Consultation with Professionals Law’s compliance experts
Collection and verification of director's documents
Preparation of DIR-3 KYC form with updated personal details
Verification of mobile and email through OTP
Filing DIR-3 KYC form with MCA using DSC
Confirmation and acknowledgment of successful filing

body.description: Non-filing leads to DIN deactivation by MCA.
Late filing attracts a penalty of ₹5,000 per DIN.
Directors with deactivated DIN cannot undertake any company-related filings or activities.

body.title: DIR-3 KYC Filing
body.description: On or before 30th September each financial year

body.title: For newly allotted DIN holders
body.description: Within the prescribed due date of the year of allotment

body.question: Who needs to file DIR-3 KYC?
body.answer: All directors or DIN holders, irrespective of their current designation or active status, must file DIR-3 KYC annually.

body.question: What happens if DIR-3 KYC is not filed?
body.answer: The DIN is deactivated and a penalty of ₹5,000 is levied for late filing.

body.question: Is DIR-3 KYC filing required even if the director is not holding any directorship?
body.answer: Yes, once a DIN is allotted, KYC filing remains mandatory.

body.question: Can foreign directors also file DIR-3 KYC?
body.answer: Yes, foreign directors with DIN are equally required to file, using valid passport and address proof.

body.question: Can DIR-3 KYC be filed without DSC?
body.answer: No. A valid Digital Signature Certificate (DSC) is mandatory for filing.

body.question: What is the difference between DIR-3 KYC and DIR-3 KYC Web?
body.answer: DIR-3 KYC Web is for directors who have filed DIR-3 KYC once; they can confirm details online without fresh documents if no changes exist.

body.question: What mobile number and email ID should be used?
body.answer: Personal mobile number and email of the director are mandatory for OTP verification.

body.question: How does Professionals Law help with DIR-3 KYC?
body.answer: Professionals Law offers complete assistance from document collection, DSC generation, verification, to timely submission and compliance tracking.

body.question: Can multiple DINs be merged through DIR-3 KYC?
body.answer: No, separate processes are involved for DIN surrender or merging. DIR-3 KYC is for updating existing DIN details.
